seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with Instructor enables reliable structured extraction for intent detection, draft generation, and attachment handling from raw email inputs. Instructor patches LLM clients to enforce Pydantic schemas, but common failures like hallucinated enums and inconsistent nested objects can lead to sending inappropriate or incorrect emails. Vex guardrails add critical verification layers for PII filtering, tone control, and recipient validation to ensure production safety.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant processes incoming emails through a multi-stage pipeline. First, Instructor extracts structured data like user intent (reply, forward, draft), recipients, attachments, and content summaries from natural language inputs. The system then generates email drafts using validated schemas, handles attachment extraction, and routes to approval workflows before sending.

Instructor's client patching ensures parseable outputs, but without additional guardrails, risks like hallucinated email addresses or inappropriate tone can propagate. Vex integrates seamlessly by wrapping these Instructor calls with verification for high-risk operations like PII detection and confidentiality checks.

The architecture supports both Python (Instructor + OpenAI) and TypeScript (InstructorJS + Vercel AI SDK) implementations, with shared Pydantic/Zod schemas for cross-language consistency.

risks.name: PII Leakage in Drafts
risks.description: Instructor may extract and include sensitive data (SSNs, emails) in generated drafts without filtering
risks.mitigation: Vex PII detection policy blocks drafts containing unredacted sensitive information

risks.name: Inappropriate Tone Detection Failure
risks.description: Model generates sarcastic/offensive replies that pass Instructor validation but fail human review
risks.mitigation: Vex sentiment analysis ensures professional tone across all generated content

risks.name: Invalid Recipient Extraction
risks.description: Hallucinated or malformed email addresses from Instructor's nested recipient parsing
risks.mitigation: Vex email format validation + domain reputation checks before send approval

risks.name: Confidentiality Breaches
risks.description: Internal-only content included in external email drafts due to extraction context loss
risks.mitigation: Vex confidentiality classifier prevents sensitive internal data in outbound emails

implementation.title: Intent Detection with Instructor
implementation.description: Parse incoming email to extract structured intent, action type, and priority using Pydantic models
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from openai import OpenAI
from typing import Literal

class EmailIntent(BaseModel):
    action: Literal['reply', 'forward', 'draft', 'archive'] = Field(..., description="User's desired email action")
    priority: Literal['low', 'medium', 'high'] = 'medium'
    recipients: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
raw_email = "Please reply to john@company.com about the Q4 budget ASAP"
intent = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    response_model=EmailIntent,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": raw_email}]
)
print(intent.action, intent.recipients)

implementation.title: Email Draft Generation
implementation.description: Generate complete email drafts with validated structure for send approval workflow
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional

class EmailDraft(BaseModel):
    subject: str
    body: str
    to: list[str]
    cc: Optional[list[str]] = None
    attachments: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
draft = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=EmailDraft,
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "Generate professional email drafts"},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Draft reply confirming Q4 budget approval for John"}
    ]
)
print(f"Subject: {draft.subject}")
print(f"To: {', '.join(draft.to)}")

vexIntegration.title: PII Filtering Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Instructor calls with Vex to block PII in generated email drafts
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel

class EmailDraft(BaseModel):
    subject: str
    body: str
    to: list[str]

@verify(policies=["block-pii", "professional-tone"])
def generate_draft(intent: str) -> EmailDraft:
    client = from_openai(OpenAI())
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        response_model=EmailDraft,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": intent}]
    )

# Usage with automatic retries/blocking
draft = generate_draft("Reply to John about SSN 123-45-6789")
# Vex blocks PII automatically

vexIntegration.title: Recipient Validation Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Verify extracted recipients before approval workflow using Vex email validation
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify, Policy
from instructor import from_openai
from typing import Literal

@verify(policies=[
    Policy(name="valid_email", prompt="Validate all email addresses are properly formatted"),
    Policy(name="domain_whitelist", prompt="Only allow company.com and partner domains")
])
def extract_recipients(raw_email: str) -> list[str]:
    client = from_openai(OpenAI())
    class Recipients(BaseModel):
        to: list[str]
    result = client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o-mini",
        response_model=Recipients,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": raw_email}]
    )
    return result.to

recipients = extract_recipients("Send to john@gmail.com, jane@company.com")
